# frustration-analyzer memory — pagb-reconstruction

- 2026-07-21 — First run. Incident: small feasibility question ("upload the bug-report log?") escalated by main thread into (a) unrequested auto-reveal-file-manager UI step, (b) publicly FILED GitHub issue proposing OAuth device-flow auth. User: "don't even think about that shit solution". Root causes ranked: (1) "reversible default" wrongly covers PUBLISHING to external-witness surfaces (issues/PRs/releases) — proposed present-then-wait rule; (2) step-9 auto-ship license has no blast-radius ceiling — proposed excluding new security/auth boundaries. Proposals routed to librarian same turn.

- 2026-07-22 — Incident (the INVERSE of 2026-07-21, and its unintended consequence): verified fixes committed+pushed to main but never TAGGED; the release CI is tag-triggered and Eloïse is release-only, so she got nothing despite standing "ship ASAP" authorization. SECOND occurrence (see [[ship-means-push-and-tag]]). Root cause: the 2026-07-21 "present-then-wait for external publications" rule was too BROAD — decide-and-surface's external-publication bullet lists "release" and CONTRADICTS execute-now's already-authorized-operational-action carve-out ("publish must not be re-asked once standing-authorized"). Agent followed the more absolute one and withheld the tag. Fix proposed + routed to librarian: a two-axis test (audience: uncontrolled third-party vs project's own known/authorized consumer × content: novel/unreviewed vs already-verified/requested) — propose-then-WAIT fires ONLY on novel+uncontrolled; the project's own tag-triggered release channel to its release-only consumer is already-authorized, so the tag-push completes the Commit step for user-visible work. Security/credential flows stay propose-then-WAIT regardless.
